q:
The catalytic converter in a car purifies the exhaust fumes.

A. Right.
B. Wrong.
e:
LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’.
The catalytic converter removes the harmful and toxic substances from the exhaust gases (nitrogen oxides, carbon monoxide, hydrocarbons).
clean_q:
The catalytic converter in a car purifies the exhaust fumes. 
A. Right.
B. Wrong.
clean_e:
LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’. 
The catalytic converter removes the harmful and toxic substances from the exhaust gases (nitrogen oxides, carbon monoxide, hydrocarbons).

q:
Your driving gear is too low.
The fuel consumption will be …

A. ... lower.
B. ... the same.
C. ... higher.
e:
LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’.
If your driving gear is too low, then the fuel consumption will be higher.
clean_q:
Your driving gear is too low.
The fuel consumption will be … 
A. ... lower.
B. ... the same.
C. ... higher.
clean_e:
LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’. 
If your driving gear is too low, then the fuel consumption will be higher.

q:
When is a car unmanageable because of aquaplaning?

A. You do not drive in the tracks of your vehicle ahead.
B. Sigh strength of a truck before you pull the car along.
C. The tires of your car have no contact with the road surface.
e:
LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’.
One speaks of aquaplaning when, because of water, the four tires cannot touch the road surface.

When is a car unmanageable because of aquaplaning?
A. You do not drive in the tracks of your vehicle ahead.
B. Sigh strength of a truck before you pull the car along.
C. The tires of your car have no contact with the road surface.
clean_e:
LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’.
One speaks of aquaplaning when, because of water, the four tires cannot touch the road surface.

C. You drive to a garage.

q: If snow chains are fitted around the tires, you may drive a maximum of (...) km / hour within a built-up area in Flanders.
e:
LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’.
In a built-up area, the maximum permitted speed is 50 km / hour.
clean_q: If snow chains are fitted around the tires, you may drive a maximum of (...) km / hour within a built-up area in Flanders.
clean_e:
LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’.
In a built-up area, the maximum permitted speed is 50 km / hour.

q:
Which answer is WRONG?
It is March 1st. It is snowing heavily in this built-up area and visibility is only 60 metres.

A. The car may use studded tyres.
B. The car may use snow chains.
C. The front and rear fog lights must be switched on.
e:
LESSON 31
The question was: “which answer is wrong.”
A. Correct. Studded tyres are allowed between 1 November and 31 March.
B. Correct. Snow chains may always be used when there is snow on the roadway.
C. Wrong. Only the rear fog lights must be switched on.
clean_q:
Which answer is WRONG?
It is March 1st. It is snowing heavily in this built-up area and visibility is only 60 metres.
A. The car may use studded tyres.
B. The car may use snow chains.
C. The front and rear fog lights must be switched on.
clean_e:
LESSON 31
The question was: “which answer is wrong.”
A. Correct. Studded tyres are allowed between 1 November and 31 March.
B. Correct. Snow chains may always be used when there is snow on the roadway.
C. Wrong. Only the rear fog lights must be switched on.

q:
What influence does too high tire pressure has on the road position?

A. The car is better positioned on the road.
B. The car is worse positioned on the road.
C. The tire pressure has no effect on this.
clean_q:
What influence does too high tire pressure has on the road position? 
A. The car is better positioned on the road.

q:
Your car starts to skid.
In which direction should you look?

A. Towards the danger.
B. Towards the direction you intended to go.
e:
LESSON 31
Although braking is probably your first instinct, you should avoid braking too hard. This can cause the wheels to lock and reduce traction.
What should you do instead?
Look in the direction you want to go. Unconsciously, you will steer towards where you are looking. You can imagine what happens if you keep looking at the danger.
Steer in the opposite direction (counter-steer).
Press the clutch and release the accelerator.
clean_q:
Your car starts to skid.
In which direction should you look?
A. Towards the danger.
B. Towards the direction you intended to go.
clean_e:
LESSON 31
Although braking is probably your first instinct, you should avoid braking too hard. This can cause the wheels to lock and reduce traction.
What should you do instead?
Look in the direction you want to go. Unconsciously, you will steer towards where you are looking. You can imagine what happens if you keep looking at the danger.
Steer in the opposite direction (counter-steer).
Press the clutch and release the accelerator.

q:
You have to drive downhill for a long distance. What should you do?

A. Let the car roll downhill in neutral.
B. Shift to a lower gear.
C. Keep driving and brake regularly.
e:
LESSON 31
Even when driving downhill, you should engage a lower gear.
Rule of thumb: descend in the same gear you would use to climb the same hill.
This allows engine braking and prevents brake wear.
clean_q:
You have to drive downhill for a long distance. What should you do?
A. Let the car roll downhill in neutral.
B. Shift to a lower gear.
C. Keep driving and brake regularly.
clean_e:
LESSON 31
Even when driving downhill, you should engage a lower gear.
Rule of thumb: descend in the same gear you would use to climb the same hill.
This allows engine braking and prevents brake wear.

q:
It is March 25th and it is snowing heavily. Visibility is 80 metres.
Is this car allowed to use studded tyres?

A. No.
B. Yes, only outside a built-up area.
C. Yes, both inside and outside built-up areas.
e:
LESSON 31
Studded tyres may be used between 1 November and 31 March, both inside and outside built-up areas.

It is March 25th and it is snowing heavily. Visibility is 80 metres.

clean_e:
LESSON 31
Studded tyres may be used between 1 November and 31 March, both inside and outside built-up areas.

q:
The marks in the grooves indicate when the minimum tread depth is reached. It is then time to change the tyre.

A. Correct.
B. False.
e: LESSON 31
clean_q:

B. False.
clean_e: LESSON 31

q:
The four digits following the word DOT on the sidewall of a tire indicate the tire’s production date.

A. The first two digits show the week, and the last two the year.
B. The first two digits show the year, and the last two the week.
C. The four digits have nothing to do with the production date.
e:
LESSON 31
The DOT code includes a 4-digit number: the first two digits indicate the week of manufacture, and the last two indicate the year. For example, 3519 means the tire was made in week 35 of 2019.
clean_q:
The four digits following the word DOT on the sidewall of a tire indicate the tire’s production date.
A. The first two digits show the week, and the last two the year.
B. The first two digits show the year, and the last two the week.
C. The four digits have nothing to do with the production date.
clean_e:
LESSON 31
The DOT code includes a 4-digit number: the first two digits indicate the week of manufacture, and the last two indicate the year. For example, 3519 means the tire was made in week 35 of 2019.

q:
May a smartphone be used as a GPS in the car?

A. No, that is forbidden.
B. Yes, always.
C. Yes, if it is placed in a fixed holder.
e:
LESSON 31
Using a smartphone as a GPS is allowed, as long as the device is placed in a fixed holder.
Of course, you may only hold the smartphone in your hand when you are stopped or parked.

May a smartphone be used as a GPS in the car?

q:
The function of motor oil.

1. Has a cleaning and lubricating function.
2. Ensures that the moving parts don’t touch each other.
3. Prevents the motor from blocking.
4. All above are correct.
e:
LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’.
Motor oil has a lubricating function and prevents overheating and blocking of the engine.
The oil protects the internal parts against rust and cleans the engine.
clean_q:
The function of motor oil.
1. Has a cleaning and lubricating function. 
2. Ensures that the moving parts don’t touch each other. 
3. Prevents the motor from blocking. 
4. All above are correct.
clean_e:
LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’. 
Motor oil has a lubricating function and prevents overheating and blocking of the engine.
The oil protects the internal parts against rust and cleans the engine.

q:
If there are radial tires on the car at the front, which tires should be at the back?

A. Radial.
B. Diagonal.
C. Radial or Diagonal.
e:
- RADIAL TIRES at the front and rear are allowed.
- Front and rear DIAGONAL TIRES is allowed.
- DIAGONAL at the front and RADIAL at the rear is allowed.
- RADIAL at the front and DIAGINAL at the rear is NOT allowed.
(because: You may only place radial tires at the front if there are also radial tires at the back.
clean_q:
If there are radial tires on the car at the front, which tires should be at the back?
A. Radial.
B. Diagonal.
C. Radial or Diagonal.
clean_e:
- RADIAL TIRES at the front and rear are allowed.
- Front and rear DIAGONAL TIRES is allowed.
- DIAGONAL at the front and RADIAL at the rear is allowed.
- RADIAL at the front and DIAGINAL at the rear is NOT allowed.
   (because: You may only place radial tires at the front if there are also radial tires at the back.

q:
One speaks of aquaplaning when, because of the water of heavy rain, the four tires cannot touch the road surface.

A. Correct.
B. Fault.
e:
LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’.
Aquaplaning: suddenly loss of contact with the road in rain. Aqua Planning is the situation that between the tires and the road surface is a thin layer of water. The result is that the car will 'float'. The car will be in effect temporarily unmanageable.
clean_q:
One speaks of aquaplaning when, because of the water of heavy rain, the four tires cannot touch the road surface.
A. Correct.
B. Fault.
clean_e:
LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’.
Aquaplaning: suddenly loss of contact with the road in rain. Aqua Planning is the situation that between the tires and the road surface is a thin layer of water. The result is that the car will 'float'. The car will be in effect temporarily unmanageable.

q:
What should you do if you notice an oil leak under your car?

A. Continue driving until the next service.
B. Go to a garage as soon as possible.
C. Check the oil level before every trip and add oil if needed.
e:
LESSON 13
An oil leak can cause serious engine damage. Even if you top up the oil regularly, the leak remains dangerous. You must have the leak repaired in a garage as soon as possible.
clean_q:
What should you do if you notice an oil leak under your car?
A. Continue driving until the next service.
B. Go to a garage as soon as possible. 
C. Check the oil level before every trip and add oil if needed.
clean_e:
LESSON 13
An oil leak can cause serious engine damage. Even if you top up the oil regularly, the leak remains dangerous. You must have the leak repaired in a garage as soon as possible.

q:
You have to turn left.
What order do you use?

A. Use the direction indicator – look in the mirrors – use the brakes.
B. Use the brakes - use the direction indicator - look in the mirrors.
C. Look in the mirrors - use the direction indicator – use the brakes.
e:
LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’.
Always first look if it can turn safely. Then turn on the turn signal. Sufficiently slow down.

You have to turn left.

clean_e:
LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’. 
Always first look if it can turn safely. Then turn on the turn signal. Sufficiently slow down.

q:
What should you do if your vehicle starts aquaplaning?

A. Brake hard and steer to regain control.
B. Release the accelerator, do not brake and keep the steering wheel straight.
C. Accelerate so that the tyres regain grip.
e:
LESSON 31
Aquaplaning is a phenomenon in which the tyres lose contact with the road because of a layer of water.
If your vehicle starts aquaplaning, release the accelerator, do not brake and keep the steering wheel as straight as possible.
Avoid sudden steering movements. Control will gradually return once the tyres regain contact with the road.
clean_q:
What should you do if your vehicle starts aquaplaning?
A. Brake hard and steer to regain control.
B. Release the accelerator, do not brake and keep the steering wheel straight.
C. Accelerate so that the tyres regain grip.
clean_e:
LESSON  31
Aquaplaning is a phenomenon in which the tyres lose contact with the road because of a layer of water.
If your vehicle starts aquaplaning, release the accelerator, do not brake and keep the steering wheel as straight as possible.
Avoid sudden steering movements. Control will gradually return once the tyres regain contact with the road.

q:
What should you do when you have to drive up a steep slope?

A. You just continue driving more slowly.
B. You shift to a higher gear.
C. You shift to a lower gear.
e:
LESSON 31
When driving steep uphill or steep downhill, it is best to shift to a lower gear.
This gives the engine more power and improves control of the vehicle.

q: If the air filter is dirty, does it increase fuel consumption?
e:
LESSON 31
A clogged or dirty air filter reduces the airflow to the engine, which increases the amount of fuel required to maintain vehicle performance.
A dirty air filter therefore has a significant impact on fuel consumption, whether the vehicle runs on petrol or diesel.
Depending on your vehicle and fuel type, the increase in consumption can range from 10% to 25%.
clean_q: If the air filter is dirty, does it increase fuel consumption?
clean_e:
LESSON 31
A clogged or dirty air filter reduces the airflow to the engine, which increases the amount of fuel required to maintain vehicle performance.
A dirty air filter therefore has a significant impact on fuel consumption, whether the vehicle runs on petrol or diesel.
Depending on your vehicle and fuel type, the increase in consumption can range from 10% to 25%.

q:
What is the function of motor oil?

A. Only lubricating the engine.
B. Only lubricating and cooling down of the engine.
C. Lubricating, cooling down and cleaning of the engine.
e:
LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’.
Motor oil has a lubricating function and prevents overheating and blocking of the engine.
The oil protects the internal parts against rust and cleans the engine.

q:
When should you check the tire pressure?

A. When the tires are warm.
B. When the tires are cold.
e:
LESSON 31 - "Technology".
You can check the tire pressure when the tires are cold and the car is horizontal.
clean_q:
When should you check the tire pressure?
A. When the tires are warm.
B. When the tires are cold.
clean_e:
LESSON 31 - "Technology".
You can check the tire pressure when the tires are cold and the car is horizontal.

q:
This light is on (ABS). What should you do?

A. You must stop immediately because your brakes no longer work.
B. You keep driving but have it checked at a garage.
C. You remove the fuse so the light stops glowing.
e:
LESSON 31
When the ABS warning light is on, it means the anti-lock braking system is not working properly. However, your normal brakes still function. You can continue driving carefully, but you should have the system inspected as soon as possible.
clean_q:
This light is on (ABS). What should you do?
A. You must stop immediately because your brakes no longer work.
B. You keep driving but have it checked at a garage.
C. You remove the fuse so the light stops glowing.
clean_e:
LESSON 31
When the ABS warning light is on, it means the anti-lock braking system is not working properly. However, your normal brakes still function. You can continue driving carefully, but you should have the system inspected as soon as possible.

q:
The windshield washer reservoir is empty. This may negatively affect the driver’s visibility.
What should you do?

A. Stop immediately and refill.
B. Drive to the nearest garage.
C. Refill as soon as possible.
e:
LESSON 31
If you hear a humming or buzzing sound when you activate the washer but no liquid sprays onto the windshield, the reservoir is empty. You should refill it as soon as possible, because without washer fluid your visibility can be greatly reduced, especially in rain or when dirt splashes up from the road. Just like engine oil, the washer fluid level should be checked regularly.
clean_q:
The windshield washer reservoir is empty. This may negatively affect the driver’s visibility.

C. Refill as soon as possible.
clean_e:
LESSON 31

You should refill it as soon as possible, because without washer fluid your visibility can be greatly reduced, especially in rain or when dirt splashes up from the road. Just like engine oil, the washer fluid level should be checked regularly.

q:
What are the consequences when your tyre pressure is too low?

A. Higher fuel consumption.
B. Quickly bad worn tyres.
C. Higher fuel consumption and quickly badly worn tyres.
e:
LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’.
You consume more fuel and the tires wear out faster.

LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’. 
You consume more fuel and the tires wear out faster.

q:
It is important to change the tyres every 10.000 or 15.000 km.
How do you have to switch them?

A. Crosswise.
B. Sun wise.
C. Per axle.
e:
LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’.
The screws must be tightened crosswise.

B. Only with studded tires.
C. With snow chains or studded tires.
e: LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’.

q:
What is NOT allowed?

A. Front and rear radial tires.
B. Radial front and diagonal rear tires.
C. Front diagonal and rear radial tires.
e:
LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’.
- RADIAL TIRES are authorized at the front and at the rear.
- DIAGONAL TIRES are authorized at the front and at the rear.
- DIAGONAL at the front and RADIAL at the rear is authorized.
- RADIAL at the front and DIAGINAL at the rear are NOT authorized.
(because: you can only put radial tires on the front if there are also radial tires on the rear.
clean_q:
What is NOT allowed?
A. Front and rear radial tires.
B. Radial front and diagonal rear tires.
C. Front diagonal and rear radial tires.
clean_e:
LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’. 
- RADIAL TIRES are authorized at the front and at the rear.
- DIAGONAL TIRES are authorized at the front and at the rear.
- DIAGONAL at the front and RADIAL at the rear is authorized.
- RADIAL at the front and DIAGINAL at the rear are NOT authorized.
(because: you can only put radial tires on the front if there are also radial tires on the rear.

q: Does the cold winter weather influence the working of the battery of a car?
e:
LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’.
When the weather is very cold, a battery has difficulties to work properly. In winter many batteries are broken.
clean_q: Does the cold winter weather influence the working of the battery of a car?
clean_e:

q:
It is 30 November.
What is the maximum permitted speed when driving with studded tyres within a built-up area in the Brussels-Capital Region?

A. Driving with studded tyres is not allowed.
B. Maximum 60 km/h.
C. Maximum 30 km/h.
e:
LESSON 31
Studded tyres may be used between 1 November and 31 March on vehicles with a maximum authorised mass up to 3.5 tonnes.
Speed limits with studded tyres:
- Motorways and roads with 2×2 lanes separated by a central reservation: maximum 90 km/h.
- Ordinary roads: maximum 60 km/h.
- Within a built-up area: the maximum speed applicable there.
In the Brussels-Capital Region, this is 30 km/h.

clean_e:

- Ordinary roads: maximum 60 km/h.
- Within a built-up area: the maximum speed applicable there.
In the Brussels-Capital Region, this is 30 km/h.

q:
To check the oil level, the car must be on a flat surface.
But when should you check the oil level?

A. When the engine is cold.
B. After driving a few kilometres so the oil is warm.
e:
LESSON 31 – ‘Technique’.
Engine oil is one of the most important fluids in your vehicle. It lubricates moving parts, prevents overheating and wear, and helps keep the engine clean.
To get a reliable reading, you should check the oil level when the engine is cold and the car is parked on a level surface. If the oil is still warm, it may not have fully drained back into the oil pan, leading to an inaccurate reading.
clean_q:
